items.title: What Tim Cook built
items.content: On the latest episode of Equity, we discuss how Apple has changed since Cook became CEO in 2011, and what challenges incoming CEO John Ternus will be facing.

items.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
items.content: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

items.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
items.content: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus. Ternus may be inheriting one

items.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
items.content: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

items.title: Microsoft offers buyout for up to 7% of US employees
items.content: If a worker's years of service at Microsoft plus their age equals 70 or more, they will be eligible for a voluntary retirement buyout.

items.title: Hackers are abusing unpatched Windows security flaws to hack into organizations
items.content: A security researcher published details of three security vulnerabilities in Windows Defender, and the code used to exploit them. Now, hackers are taking advantage of the vuln

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.content: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connectio

items.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
items.content: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

items.title: United Airlines Says It Pitched American on Merger but Was Rebuffed
items.content: United’s chief acknowledged for the first time that he had broached the idea of a merger with American, which was unwilling to consider it.

items.title: Musk vs. Altman: A High-Stakes A.I. Clash Goes to Court on Monday
items.content: Elon Musk is seeking more than $150 billion in damages and a complete shake-up of OpenAI. The outcome could have big consequences for the artificial intelligence industry.

items.title: China’s Economy Starts to Show Cracks From Iran War
items.content: China’s strategic reserves of oil and natural gas have insulated it somewhat, but its manufacturing-based economy is beginning to falter.

items.title: China Will Require Meta to Unwind Acquisition of AI Start-Up Manus
items.content: The impact of the ruling was not immediately clear, but it could send a chilling signal to Chinese tech founders seeking to team up with foreign companies.
